description_long: This is a slender, elongated amphibian with a smooth, moist skin and a long, tapering tail. Its body is dark brown to black with a distinct orange or reddish stripe running laterally from the head to the tip of the tail. The limbs are short and splayed, each ending in small, delicate toes. The head is broad and slightly flattened, with visible eyes and a rounded snout. The underside appears lighter, with hints of orange or red coloration. This animal is well-adapted for both aquatic and terrestrial environments, often found near water sources in urban parks or gardens.
description: A slender, dark brown amphibian with an orange lateral stripe and a long tail, featuring short limbs and a broad head.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, the orange lateral stripe, and the elongated tail.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the broad head, splayed limbs, and the symmetry of the orange stripe along the back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the orange stripe and the smooth, streamlined body.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the lighter underside and the arrangement of the limbs and tail.
